commands.review_summary:
The PR updates `OpenClawChatTimeline` to render user-bubble text through selectable inlines with a cached selection highlight brush and keep running multi-step tool bursts collapsed with an aggregate Running chip.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main uses `TextBlock.Text` for selectable user bubbles and switches running Auto tool bursts to Plain. I did not run the WinUI tray locally in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed because there are no concrete blocking findings; maintainers should review and let required checks gate the current head.

target.title: Fix native chat UI bugs in tray app
target.body:
Three bugs in the WinUI 3 native chat timeline.

### 1. Collapsed tool-burst groups momentarily expanded mid-stream
Each time a new tool started running inside a collapsed group, the `CompactSummary` briefly auto-expanded to show the running tool, then re-collapsed. Now the group stays collapsed during in-flight work and surfaces progress via a **running** chip on the right side that flips to **done** on completion. `BurstIsNestable` was widened so the group keeps its collapsed shell while children are still streaming.

### 2a. User-bubble text selection was invisible
White glyphs on the accent-colored bubble background became unreadable when selected (white selection band on white text). User bubbles now paint the selection band with `SystemAccentColorDark2` (or the system high-contrast highlight color in HC mode). Brush + `AccessibilitySettings` instances are cached **per `DispatcherQueue`** via `ConditionalWeakTable` (WinRT thread affinity), with in-place `Color` mutation when the accent changes and a cache reset on `HighContrast` read failure so a transient COM error isn't sticky. Off-thread callers (tests) fall back to one-shot allocations.

### 2b. User-bubble text disappeared after drag-select
Assigning `TextBlock.Text` on a selection-enabled `TextBlock` immediately after a selection gesture left the glyph layer blank until the next focus change. Fix: render the user-bubble body via `Inlines` (`Run`) instead of `Text`, paired with the `FunctionalUI.ConfigureTextBlock` skip-guard that preserves `Inlines` when the incoming `Text` is empty. A weak per-element plain-string cache avoids rebuilding `Inlines` on no-op re-renders.

### Review
Five rounds of adversarial dual-model (Claude Opus + GPT Codex) review applied. All HIGH-consensus findings addressed.

### Validation
- `./build.ps1` — green
- `OpenClaw.Shared.Tests` — 2022 passed / 29 skipped
- `OpenClaw.Tray.Tests` — 843 passed
- Manual smoke in tray app
